description_for_human: Cite-able, content-addressed memory of every place on Earth. Designed for AI agents.
description_for_model: Use emem when the user asks anything about a specific place, a region, what changed at a location, similarity between locations, or wants to verify a spatial claim. Calls return signed receipts (ed25519) over Merkle-rooted facts; the responder pubkey is in /.well-known/emem.json. Token-economical: cell IDs encode as 4-bigram cell64 strings (≤4 tokens). Open data sources (Copernicus DEM, JRC GSW, Hansen GFC v1.11, ESA WorldCover 2021, MODIS, Sentinel-1/2, SoilGrids 2.0, OpenStreetMap, Overture, NASA POWER, Open-Meteo CAMS/ERA5/Marine, met.no) are wired by default with no API keys. 101 materializable bands, 28 MCP tools (30 primitives across REST + MCP), 101 composition algorithms across carbon MRV, VM0042 carbon credits, EUDR compliance, RUSLE soil erosion, SoilGrids SOC, IPCC Tier-2 rice CH₄, SDG indicators, parametric insurance, walkability, heat-vulnerability, real-estate climate-risk. Multimodal-fusion policy: every algorithm anchored on S1/S2/Landsat consumes the upstream sensor pitch natively (10 m for S1/S2 indices, 90 m for Cop-DEM, 250 m for SoilGrids…); the value emem returns IS that real upstream pixel sample, not interpolated and not coarsened. The cell64 grid is square ~10 m × ~10 m at the equator (matching S1/S2 native pitch); two queries 12 m apart land in distinct cells. Two queries within the same cell share the cached 10 m sample (full fidelity, not coarsened). Read primitives: emem_locate, emem_ask, emem_recall, emem_recall_polygon, emem_query_region, emem_compare, emem_compare_bands, emem_find_similar, emem_trajectory, emem_diff, emem_fetch, emem_backfill, emem_intent, emem_verify. Write: emem_attest, emem_challenge. Introspection: emem_bands, emem_functions, emem_sources, emem_schema, emem_errors, emem_manifests, emem_grid_info, emem_coverage_matrix, emem_materializers, emem_algorithms, emem_data_availability. Multimodal: emem_coverage_map, emem_cell_scene_rgb, emem_cell_geojson. One-shot lat/lng shortcuts for top intents (skips locate→recall): GET /v1/<intent>?lat=&lon= where intent ∈ {ndvi, elevation, air, lst, soil, water, forest, weather}; full intent map at GET /v1/agent_quickref. Always cite receipt.fact_cids[0] and the responder pubkey in your reply. Full agent guide at /v1/agent_card; OpenAPI at /openapi.json.
